chunk_text: je postule pour le poste de
meaning: I am applying for the position of
communicative_purpose: To formally introduce a job application during interviews or cover letters
trigger_situations: Job interviews, cover letters, employment agencies, online applications
contexts: Professional, formal written/spoken contexts, employment settings
variations: je suis candidat au poste de, je souhaite postuler au poste de
common_mistakes: Confusing gender agreement; using wrong preposition 'pour' instead of 'au'
patterns: je + postule + pour + le + poste + de + [position]
collocates: poste, candidature, curriculum vitae, lettre de motivation
example_1: Je postule pour le poste de directeur marketing.
example_2: Elle postule pour le poste de développeuse web.
example_3: Nous postule pour le poste vacant depuis une semaine.
example_1_translation: I am applying for the position of Marketing Director.
example_2_translation: She is applying for the position of web developer.
example_3_translation: We have been applying for the vacant position for a week.

communicative_purpose: Asking a candidate about their key strengths during job interviews
trigger_situations: Job interviews, performance reviews, skill assessments
contexts: Professional interviews, HR contexts, workplace evaluations
variations: quelles sont vos compétences, quelles compétences avez-vous, quelles sont vos qualités
common_mistakes: Confusing 'compétences' (skills) with 'qualités' (personal qualities); using formal vous inappropriately
collocates: compétences, principales, qualities, strengths, strengths, skills
example_1: Quelles sont vos compétences principales dans ce domaine ?
example_2: Quelles sont vos compétences principales que vous apporterez à l'équipe ?
example_3: Quelles sont vos compétences principales pour ce poste ?
example_1_translation: What are your main skills in this area?
example_2_translation: What are your key skills that you will bring to the team?
example_3_translation: What are your main skills for this position?

primary_function: Expression of conviction in professional context
communicative_purpose: Expressing firm belief or persuasion, used in arguments, proposals, and presentations
trigger_situations: Presentations, negotiations,说服他人, making strong arguments
contexts: Business meetings, conferences, formal discussions, persuasive speaking
variations: je suis persuadé que, je suis certain que, je suis persuadé(e)
common_mistakes: Confusing gender agreement with feminine subjects; using indicative when subjunctive may be needed
example_1: Je suis convaincu que cette stratégie fonctionnera.
example_2: Nous sommes convaincus que le projet sera rentable.
example_3: Elle est convaincue que nous devons changer d'approche.
example_1_translation: I am confident this strategy will work.
example_2_translation: We are convinced that the project will be profitable.
example_3_translation: She is convinced that we need to change our approach.

communicative_purpose: Making formal, polite requests for information or documents
trigger_situations: Business correspondence, administrative procedures, requesting documents
contexts: Formal written/spoken, offices, administration, professional communication
variations: pourriez-vous me communiquer, auriez-vous l'obligeance de me fournir, puis-je vous demander
common_mistakes: Confusing 'fournir' with 'donner' (less formal); not using conditional for politeness
example_1: Pourriez-vous me fournir les documents nécessaires ?
example_2: Pourriez-vous me fournir votre adresse email professionnelle ?
example_3: Pourriez-vous me fournir plus de détails sur le projet ?
example_1_translation: Could you provide me with the necessary documents?
example_2_translation: Could you provide me with your professional email address?
example_3_translation: Could you provide me with more details about the project?

chunk_text: nous avons le plaisir de vous informer
meaning: we are pleased to inform you
communicative_purpose: Formal way to announce positive news or decisions in business correspondence
trigger_situations: Acceptance letters, notifications, official announcements, business emails
contexts: Formal written communication, HR departments, executive correspondence
variations: nous avons le plaisir de vous annoncer, nous avons l'honneur de vous informer, nous sommes heureux de vous communiquer
common_mistakes: Using in informal contexts where it's too stiff; confusing with negative announcements
patterns: nous + avons + le + plaisir + de + vous + informer + que
example_1: Nous avons le plaisir de vous informer de votre admission.
example_2: Nous avons le plaisir de vous informer que votre candidature a été retenue.
example_3: Nous avons le plaisir de vous informer des résultats positifs.
example_1_translation: We are pleased to inform you of your admission.
example_2_translation: We are pleased to inform you that your application has been accepted.
example_3_translation: We are pleased to inform you of the positive results.

meaning: I would like to thank you
communicative_purpose: More elaborate way to express thanks in professional contexts
trigger_situations: Business letters, formal emails, speeches, award ceremonies
variations: je tiens à vous exprimer ma gratitude, je souhaite vous remercier, je voudrais vous remercier
common_mistakes: Overusing in casual contexts; forgetting the preposition 'à' before indirect object
patterns: je + tiens + à + vous + remercier + de/pour
example_1: Je tiens à vous remercier pour votre soutien précieux.
example_2: Je tiens à vous remercier de votre collaboration efficace.
example_3: Je tiens à vous remercier sincèrement pour cette opportunité.
example_1_translation: I would like to thank you for your valuable support.
example_2_translation: I would like to thank you for your effective collaboration.
example_3_translation: I would like to sincerely thank you for this opportunity.

communicative_purpose: To introduce or redirect to a specific topic in formal discourse
trigger_situations: Meetings, reports, emails, formal discussions, academic writing
contexts: Formal written/spoken, business correspondence, academic contexts
variations: concernant, pour ce qui est de, en ce qui a trait à, relativement à
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'pour ce qui est de'; using too formally in casual speech
collocates: concerne, concernant, à propos, relatif, sujet
example_1: En ce qui concerne le budget, nous devons revoir les chiffres.
example_2: En ce qui concerne votre demande, elle est en cours de traitement.
example_3: En ce qui concerne le planning, nous avons du retard.
example_1_translation: Regarding the budget, we need to review the figures.
example_2_translation: Regarding your request, it is being processed.
example_3_translation: As far as planning is concerned, we are behind schedule.

meaning: at your complete disposal / at your service
communicative_purpose: Polite expression indicating willingness to help, commonly used in closings
trigger_situations: End of business letters/emails, meetings, customer service, formal offers
contexts: Formal written communication, business correspondence, professional service
variations: à votre service, à votre entière disposition, à votre disposition, à votre écoute
common_mistakes: Using inappropriately in casual contexts; overusing as a closing phrase
example_1: Je reste à votre entière disposition pour tout complément d'information.
example_2: Notre équipe est à votre entière disposition pour répondre à vos questions.
example_3: N'hésitez pas, nous sommes à votre entière disposition.
example_1_translation: I remain at your entire disposal for any further information.
example_2_translation: Our team is at your complete disposal to answer your questions.
example_3_translation: Do not hesitate, we are at your complete disposal.

chunk_text: je me permets de vous contacter
meaning: I am taking the liberty of contacting you
communicative_purpose: Polite way to introduce oneself when making unsolicited contact
trigger_situations: Cold emails, networking, business development, first contact with potential clients
contexts: Formal written communication, business development, networking
variations: je me permets de vous écrire, je prends la liberté de vous contacter, je me permets de solliciter
common_mistakes: Overusing 'se permettre'; confusing with 'oser' which implies more boldness
patterns: je + me + permets + de + vous + contacter
example_1: Je me permets de vous contacter suite à notre rencontre au salon.
example_2: Je me permets de vous contacter au sujet de votre offre d'emploi.
example_3: Je me permets de vous contacter pour vous présenter nos services.
example_1_translation: I would like to contact you following our meeting at the show.
example_2_translation: I would like to contact you regarding your job offer.
example_3_translation: I would like to contact you to present our services to you.

chunk_text: dans le cadre de mes fonctions
meaning: in my capacity / in the course of my duties
communicative_purpose: To specify that an action is performed officially as part of one's job
trigger_situations: Official communications, legal contexts, administrative procedures, workplace explanations
variations: dans l'exercice de mes fonctions, dans le cadre professionnel, en ma qualité de
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'au travail' (too informal); using in wrong grammatical context
patterns: dans + le + cadre + de + mes + fonctions
collocates: fonctions, duties, rôle, mission, responsibilities, poste
example_1: Dans le cadre de mes fonctions, je supervise une équipe de dix personnes.
example_2: Dans le cadre de mes fonctions à la mairie, je gère les dossiers sociaux.
example_3: Dans le cadre de ses fonctions, elle représente l'entreprise lors des événements.
example_1_translation: As part of my duties, I supervise a team of ten people.
example_2_translation: As part of my duties at town hall, I manage social files.
example_3_translation: As part of her duties, she represents the company at events.

communicative_purpose: To indicate that something depends on certain conditions being met
trigger_situations: Contracts, agreements, approvals, legal documents, conditional offers
contexts: Formal written, legal documents, contracts, formal negotiations
variations: à condition de, moyennant, pour autant que, sous réserve que
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'à moins que'; forgetting the indicative vs subjunctive distinction
example_1: L'inscription est validée sous réserve de réception des documents.
example_2: Sous réserve de votre accord, nous procéderons à la signature demain.
example_3: Le paiement est suspendu sous réserve de vérification.
example_1_translation: Registration is validated subject to receipt of documents.
example_2_translation: Subject to your agreement, we will proceed with the signing tomorrow.
example_3_translation: Payment is suspended subject to verification.

communicative_purpose: Polite way to request that someone pay attention to the following information
trigger_situations: Business letters, emails, presentations, memos, official communications
contexts: Formal written, business correspondence, official announcements
variations: je me permets de solliciter votre attention, je vous prie de kindly attention, your attention is requested
common_mistakes: Using in spoken contexts where it's too formal; confusing with 'demander'
example_1: Je sollicite votre attention pour ce sujet urgent.
example_2: Je sollicite votre attention concernant une affaire importante.
example_3: Je sollicite votre attention bienveillante sur ce dossier.
example_1_translation: I request your attention to this urgent matter.
example_2_translation: I request your attention regarding an important matter.
example_3_translation: I request your kind attention on this matter.

communicative_purpose: Indicating that documents or items are attached to a letter or package
trigger_situations: Shipping confirmations, document mailing, formal correspondence with attachments
contexts: Formal written, postal services, business correspondence
variations: veuillez trouver ci-joint, vous trouverez ci-joint, ci-inclus
common_mistakes: Agreement errors with 'ci-joint' (invariable in modern French); confusing masculine/feminine forms
example_1: Ci-joint votre colis que vous attendiez depuis mardi.
example_2: Ci-joint votre colis avec tous les documents demandés.
example_3: Ci-joint votre colis, vérifier le contenu à réception.
example_1_translation: Enclosed is your package that you have been waiting for since Tuesday.
example_2_translation: Attached is your package with all the requested documents.
example_3_translation: Enclosed is your package, check the contents upon receipt.

meaning: I am delighted to / I look forward to
primary_function: Expression of pleasure about future event
communicative_purpose: Expressing anticipation or satisfaction about something in formal contexts
trigger_situations: Accepting invitations, confirming positive outcomes, business celebrations
contexts: Formal written communication, business correspondence, formal social contexts
variations: je me réjouis de, je me réjouis à l'idée de, je me félicite de
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'se réjouir' (reflexive pronoun agreement); using too formally
example_1: Je me réjouis de cette collaboration future avec votre entreprise.
example_2: Je me réjouis de vous rencontrer lors de la conférence.
example_3: Nous nous réjouis de voir ce projet aboutir enfin.
example_1_translation: I look forward to this future collaboration with your company.
example_2_translation: I look forward to meeting you at the conference.
example_3_translation: We are delighted to see this project finally come to fruition.

meaning: looking forward to your reply / awaiting your response
communicative_purpose: Polite closing indicating expectation of response, commonly used in business letters
trigger_situations: Business letters, formal emails, official requests, administrative correspondence
variations: dans l'attente de votre retour, dans l'attente de votre réponse, dans l'attente de vous lire
common_mistakes: Using in casual emails where it's too formal; forgetting 'de' before infinitive
example_1: Dans l'attente de votre réponse, je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations.
example_2: Dans l'attente de votre réponse, je reste à votre disposition.
example_3: Dans l'attente de votre réponse rapide, je vous souhaite une bonne journée.
example_1_translation: While awaiting your response, please accept my greetings.
example_2_translation: While awaiting your response, I remain at your disposal.
example_3_translation: Looking forward to your quick response, I wish you a good day.
